name: Little Witch Academia: VR Broom Racing

reviews.review_description: We bought this game a while ago, played it once and now it never loads. The screen stays black. Please help.
This game rules

reviews.review_title: Could be ok if updated, feels unfinished
reviews.review_description:
Clearly this game is aimed at kids - my daughter loves it. However that can’t excuse putting out a bare-bones game that feels unfinished for the (extremely high) price.

If this was £8.99 it might be reasonable value and heck maybe they would get a lot more sales. But at the full price it’s a bit of a joke I’m afraid. You’re paying a price for an AAA game and getting an extended demo.

The racing is OK and quite fun actually. But there’s just not enough content here. The multiplayer feels half baked - it’s fun to fly with someone else but literally nothing to do other than fly around a very limited area. You can’t even explore all the landmarks as you hit invisible walls and are quickly forcibly put back into the map. Soon gets old.

So if the devs are listening to this please please give us an update. Some Quest 2 graphical updates are badly needed (awful pop up when you move your head, even if stationary!). Some additional locations to race in. And ability to actually do some proper racing with a friend.

If it’s updated it might be a 3 or 4 star game. As it is I’ll probably be refunding it sadly as the value is just not there.

I cannot begin to describe how good this title is!

The 3D anime aesthetic is great, the visual novel-like dialogue is fully voiced (make sure to set voices to 🇯🇵 and subtitles to 🇬🇧 for best experience) and the flying itself is super fun!

I thought free-moving VR games gave nausea, but apparently I was wrong, because this game gives me absolutely none.

Leaving ★★★★★ because this is exactly the kind of official anime game I love to see!

If I had to find a flaw, it would be the graphics that are a bit low-poly. I guess it's a limitation that comes with the platform, especially if they want to be compatible with Quest 1. But it doesn't detract from the gameplay at all.

The game is pretty good I bought it because I loved the shows but I have had my oculus for around 5 month now and it’s one of the games that have a lot of improving 
(Note if you like the series and your willing to spend 40 dollars go for it but if you don’t like anime or you you like really good graphics this won’t really be for you.)

This game is lit bro- anyway sometimes i rage quit because when im flying(start) im looking 
forwards and it just keeps on telling me to look forwards and its not moving, but overall its really gooooooooood 😃or im just doing something worng..

reviews.review_title: Game is lovely but no way to restart :-(
reviews.review_description:
Played the game myself first and would now like to let my daughter play. But unlike other games- there's no way to create another user, or start from the beginning. 
This is very disappointing as it means that for a full price game, only one person can ever play it... 
She could of course chose the different levels, but everything I've done is already there and it's not the same when you are not building it up yourself.
Disappointing

reviews.review_title: Super fun. Wish more still played
reviews.review_description: So fun & true to series. Took some serious getting used to with the controls. In fact, I'm still kinda learning new tricks. Overall, it's fun and enjoyable to revisit my favorite characters & moments from the series. New updates/courses would be fantastic

I've tried other VR games where you can move or fly and I got sick which made me really nervous about plunking down for this game. There's a lot of game outside of the races so if you like anime, there's lots of scenes to watch. I managed to play the races without getting too sick. It's easy to wanna play more, but if you rest every few races you will be fine. Runs really well on Quest!

This game stood out of the Quest 2 launch line up for it's nice cartoon stylings and overall appeal. 

Visually, it's quite a treat. I'm quite a fan of the bright colour palette and sparkly effects. The gameplay is well executed, even if it is a bit simple. 
Perhaps this game is based on a children's show. Younger players are sure to be delighted to fly around. If you fly off the glowing road, you will be placed back on it. Very forgiving, which is great for youngsters or new gamers. 
I'm definitely a fan now!

You can play standing or sitting, you don't need lots of space. Fun, feels like you're going fast but not out of control. 
Shooting the ghosts can be hard and I got a bit sick trying to shoot them when they moved to my side but otherwise comfortable!
Amazing sense of speed w/o getting sick.

First of all I love Little Witch Acadmia and I hope that buying this game will show Trigger they've got to make a sequel!

Some of the other anime license VR games out there are pretty weak on gameplay and LWA VR succeeds there. The flight controls were super intuitive and easy to understand. 
LWA has a simple and charming art style that translates really well to the Quest. Luna Nova looked like it came right out of the anime. Sucy's World looks good and the game original area is really pretty!
I hope they add some more locations.

This is an amazing game for people who love the series, it's super fun but there are a few minor inconveniences. 
For example:
The characters' mouths don't move when they are talking on the broom, 
There are no male avatar options (However that is expected since Luna Nova is a school for witches, not wizards) , 
The graphics are quite low but I didn't mind it at all,
There barely any people on the online play 
And finally, after you complete the game, it gets pretty boring. 
But i am still rating it 4 stars because it was really fun to play while doing the races with various characters from the series and the animation is really good!
Thanks for English voice actors. I think you can try improve graphics, I believe oculus quest2 can handle it. Another game to my VR collection.
It's a really good and fun game put my childhood dreams into reality living in anime and flying but my only complaint is that it's too short, I wish you were only a lot longer

I really love this game.To be honest I don’t know why the mouths don’t move when your on the broom?You get to collect crystals and race other players.Even though the fly online part is not that good I still love it!I really like how the creator did the animation and the character design!I think the avatar editor could be better but no matter what I still love this game.

I really hate to give this game a 3-Star review. This is seriously one of my favorite shows and it pains me to have to do this but I need to be fair.
This game is fun. Really fun, I would love to play it for hours but there are a lot of problems that I noticed.

 I've noticed that the textures and meshes on literally every character will clip through each other and it's honestly giving me a headache. I think the reason why is because there's always a wind speed on the track, even if you're not actually moving. It's only noticeable on other racers before the race and... well, its MORE than noticeable. I just cant stop staring at it.

There's no in-game settings menu. I like going back and forth between the languages and sometimes I just plain forget to change it before I press the start button. On top of that, theres no way to quit the game without having to force crash it from the oculus menu. You could put the trigger for that as the bed in your dorm room, with an EXIT GAME function in it because it's your bed, it makes sense that you would exit the game from your BED.

There's NO SKIP FUNCTION. The races are super fun and I love replaying levels to try and get a better score. I thought that, maybe, the reason why I couldn't skip cutscenes was because I didnt finish the story. That had to be it! Nope. Finished the game, watched the credits, tried playing another level and I couldn't SKIP THE CUTSCENE. I spent like 2 to 3 minutes, which might sound nitpicky but when you cant skip dialogue those minutes start to get extremely boring, just going through a singular cutscene. 

You need to be on a swiveling chair to play. There's a lot of spinning in this game with its turns. You can't just lean to turn the broom so you literally have to spin in place which will make a lot of people dizzy when standing up. It even made me dizzy. So, either the right joystick should be added for broom turning or make it so just leaning could be a nice wide turn (since sharp turns on a fast moving broom really shouldn't be a thing). Eg, leaning right while looking slightly upwards would make you turn right because of velocity.

Loading screens. Jeez, the sheer black against the bright weight text and the picture example above it makes my brain want to die. It hurts to look at the loading screens so much, I have to either close my eyes or just plain look away from it until the level loads in. A better loading screen design would be so helpful from keeping people from getting headaches.

Not enough level types/not enough character races. There are a lot of racing levels. My favorite type of level is the ghost hunting levels. They're so fun! I can shoot ghost with my wand while trying not to crash on the track; it's pretty perfect! Sadly, there are about 4 of them out of the total 13 normal racing levels. On top of that, most of the races are with Akko. A lot of the characters like Sucy and Lotte only have a singular level aside from the actual Stella Cup.

THE ENDING. Spoiler territory!! Oh my gosh, the ending is so cliche. The characters just kept droning on about how we'll be losing this other character for an ungodly amount of time and the character doing the actual dying drones on about how they're sacrificing themselves for like 2 minutes! And on top of that, when they turn into  broom, you dont even get to RIDE that broom! I was so excited because her broom design was super cute and I thought we unlocked a new story broom or something. nope. Just a cutsccene.the story was extremely anticlimactic and it really saddened me.

The items. omg, the track items are so few and the way they're positioned on the track is just annoying. The traps are so big that they're honestly hard to dodge and see what's behind them so I can not go off the the track. My opinion, get rid of the traps on the track and make it so only other players/NPCs can drop them for rivals to ram into and get caught. dont make them so big that they're bocking the whole track.

I would say more but this review is causing my phone to lag and others have already mentioned what else I have to say. please, address these things with your game! Itll make the community thank you and make the game run so much easier.

reviews.review_title: Finally I can be a witch at Luna Nova!
reviews.review_description:
I just started playing it last night. This game is pretty good! The graphics aren't amazing, but it's decent enough and the character models look quite nice. It's awesome to be in some of the environments from the show, not to mention the flying is a lot of fun! There's also plenty of moments of dialogue/story between the main cast (with their English voice actors too!) so that's a plus!

Overall, it could use a bit of polish I think (and more customization options for the personal avatar), but I'm enjoying it a lot!

While I love the anime series with all of my heart, you are better off waiting for a sale. Here's why:

What I liked:
- The English voice actors reprising their roles is incredibly welcome
- Interacting with the characters is a charming sight. 
- The feeling of flying a broom across some of the magical landscapes from the anime is fan-servicey at best.
- Level challenges and equipment buying provide replayability.
- Some tracks have diverging paths, some less obvious than others. Plays into a risk vs reward as shorter routes have more danger.

Kids and fans of the anime may get a kick out of the world and its charm, but as a game itself, it is rough around the edges.

What I disliked:
- Incredibly repetitive.
- Lacking any sort of gameplay depth.
- Only two power-ups (mainly to make yourself faster) and one attack debuff. 
- Encourages replayability, but doesn't have any cutscene skipping for any level.
- Is only tailored for single-player since online multiplayer racing would be broken with the current mechanics. Online MP is only getting an exploration mode.
- Some characters only have one level, so if you're looking to max their friendship, you have to replay their level nine more times.
- Buying equipment can be cheesed, as I mainly waited for better gear to arrive in stock.
- Final level requires beating it seven times with seven different characters. It ruins the pacing.
- Only three ghost hunt levels and 10 racing levels.
- Levels are mainly story-based, so there isn't a "free play" where you can customize difficulty, pick your own partner, and choose the tracks (ala. Mario Kart).
- Controls are finnicky, as I have to physically swivel my entire body after making tight turns in some tracks. Broom turning sensitivity should be an option.
